sections.biography-source-undefined:
Lu Yin, styled Jingzong, was from Wu county, Wu commandery. He was a younger brother of Lu Kai. He was distantly related to and one generation below Lu Xun.

He was affiliated with the faction supporting Sun He against Sun Ba. Previously, Sun He treated Lu Yin with great respect. As the inner turmoil between Sun He and Sun Ba escalated, Lu Yin was slandered by the partisans of Sun Ba, among them Yang Zhu, and so he was thrown into prison. He was tortured to disclose any possible information, but Lu Yin remained steadfast. He was eventually released.

full_text:
Lu Yin (Jingzong) 陸胤 (敬宗)
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Lu Yin (陸胤), styledJingzong (敬宗), was born in from Wu in Wu (modern-day Suzhou in Jiangsu). Favoured by Sun He, defeated Zhao Yu and pacified Jiaozhou in 248. Lu Yin was affiliated with the Wu Kingdom.

BiographySource Undefined
Lu Yin, styled Jingzong, was from Wu county, Wu commandery. He was a younger brother of Lu Kai. He was distantly related to and one generation below Lu Xun.
He was affiliated with the faction supporting Sun He against Sun Ba. Previously, Sun He treated Lu Yin with great respect. As the inner turmoil between Sun He and Sun Ba escalated, Lu Yin was slandered by the partisans of Sun Ba, among them Yang Zhu, and so he was thrown into prison. He was tortured to disclose any possible information, but Lu Yin remained steadfast. He was eventually released.
